whatItDoes: Converts text to Unicode code points (U+0048 format) and vice versa. Enter plain text to see each character's code point, or enter code points to decode them back to readable text. Supports both U+XXXX and 0xXXXX notation for input.
whyItExists: Developers working with internationalization, character encoding issues, or Unicode escapes need to quickly look up or construct code points. This tool makes it trivial without consulting external references.
whoShouldUse: Frontend developers working with i18n, backend developers debugging encoding issues, security researchers analyzing Unicode-based attacks, and anyone working with special characters or emoji code points.
useCases: Find the Unicode code point for special characters or emoji to use in source code
Decode a sequence of Unicode escape sequences into readable text
Inspect hidden Unicode characters in suspicious text
Generate Unicode escape sequences for use in JSON or JavaScript strings
instructions: Select the conversion direction: Text to Unicode or Unicode to Text
Enter text or code points in the input field
Click Copy to copy the result to your clipboard
bestPractices: Use space-separated U+XXXX format for clean, readable output
Non-BMP characters (like emoji) use surrogate pairs in some systems but full code points here
Copy the output directly into JavaScript or JSON escape sequences
commonMistakes: Forgetting that emoji and non-Latin characters have code points above U+FFFF
Using decimal instead of hexadecimal when entering code points manually
Confusing UTF-16 surrogate pairs with Unicode code points
faq: Does this handle emoji code points? — Yes, including multi-byte code points like U+1F600.
What input formats are accepted for Unicode to Text? — U+XXXX, 0xXXXX, #XXXX, or plain hex values, space or comma separated.
Is the output safe for use in JSON strings? — Yes, the U+XXXX format can be directly used in JSON and JavaScript Unicode escapes.
